document: 1. FSCA Conduct Standard 1 of 2025 - Requirements for FIs providing FE initiatives

sections.heading: Definitions
sections.title: Definitions of key terms in financial education initiatives
sections.summary: This section defines critical terms such as 'financial education initiative', 'target group', and 'service provider' to clarify scope and applicability. It specifies that financial education initiatives must exclude marketing activities and focus on improving financial literacy. The definitions also establish that financial institutions must include potential customers in the scope of 'financial customers' and align with broader economic empowerment frameworks.

requirements.text: Authority means the Financial Sector Conduct Authority as defined in section 1 of the Act
Evaluation means a systematic and objective assessment of an ongoing or completed financial education initiative
Financial customer includes potential financial customers
Financial education aims to improve understanding of financial products and risks
Financial education initiative excludes marketing activities for specific financial products
Financial Sector Code refers to codes of good practice on broad-based black economic empowerment
Monitoring means regular collection and analysis of information about initiative progress
Service provider includes agents with arrangements for financial education initiatives
Specific financial product or service means actual financial products provided by providers
Target group includes youth, women, small businesses, and other vulnerable groups

sections.source_quote: In this Schedule, "the Act" means the Financial Sector Regulation Act, 2017 () and any word or expression to which a meaning has been assigned in the Act bears the meaning so assigned to it, and unless the context otherwise indicates –

sections.heading: Application, purpose and proportional application
sections.title: Scope and purpose of financial education initiatives
sections.summary: This section specifies that the Conduct Standard applies only when financial institutions provide financial education initiatives, including through service providers. It aims to protect financial customers by ensuring initiatives support informed financial decisions and sound governance practices. Financial institutions must take reasonable steps to ensure appropriate standards of behavior and oversight in developing, implementing, monitoring, and evaluating initiatives.

requirements.text: This Conduct Standard applies only where a financial institution provides or offers a financial education initiative
The purpose is to protect financial customers by ensuring initiatives support informed financial decisions
Financial institutions must take reasonable steps to ensure appropriate standards of behavior and oversight

sections.source_quote: This Conduct Standard is only applicable where a financial institution provides or offers a financial education initiative, including the provision of a financial education initiative by a service provider.

sections.heading: Governance arrangements
sections.title: Governance and oversight for financial education initiatives
sections.summary: Financial institutions must establish governance arrangements to oversee the design and implementation of financial education initiatives. These arrangements must include ongoing monitoring and review of initiative effectiveness. Institutions must also implement systems for reporting to the Authority and ensure employees and service providers have necessary skills and expertise. Responsibility for regulatory compliance remains with the financial institution regardless of service provider involvement.

requirements.text: Financial institution must have appropriate governance arrangements to oversee design and implementation
Governance arrangements must include ongoing monitoring and review of initiative effectiveness
Financial institution must have systems for reporting pre- and post-implementation to Authority
Financial institution must ensure employees and service providers have necessary skills and expertise
Governance arrangements must apply to service providers to ensure initiative effectiveness
Financial institution retains responsibility for all regulatory obligations regardless of service provider

sections.source_quote: A financial institution must have appropriate governance arrangements in place to oversee the design and implementation of financial education initiatives.

sections.heading: Design and development requirements for financial education initiatives
sections.title: Content and delivery requirements for financial education initiatives
sections.summary: Financial education initiatives must aim to enable individuals to make informed financial decisions and encourage positive behavior. Content must be based on objective information, targeted to the appropriate audience, distinct from marketing activities, and brand agnostic. Delivery platforms must be appropriate for the target group to ensure effective reach and relevance.

requirements.text: Financial education initiatives should enable natural persons to make informed financial decisions
Financial education initiatives should encourage positive customer behavior and influence behavioral change
Financial institution may use any delivery platform provided it is appropriate for target group
Content must be based on objective and impartial information
Content must be targeted suitably at the appropriate target group
Content must be distinct from brand-specific financial product or service sales processes
Content must be brand agnostic but may unpack financial concepts

sections.source_quote: Financial education initiative provided by a financial institution should aim to enable natural persons and small, medium and micro-enterprises to make more informed decisions, about their own finances and financial well-being or the finances and financial well-being of others that fall under their responsibility;

sections.heading: Location and logistics
sections.title: Implementation and measurement requirements for financial education initiatives
sections.summary: Financial institutions must ensure appropriate locations for face-to-face initiatives and virtual platforms for online initiatives. They must develop contingency plans for unforeseen circumstances. Outcomes must be measurable to demonstrate effectiveness and impact, with measurement methods directly tied to initiative goals. Institutions must monitor and evaluate initiatives to assess effectiveness, identify improvements, and collect data for future programs.

requirements.text: Face-to-face initiatives must use appropriate locations for effectiveness
Virtual initiatives must ensure virtual platforms are appropriate for effectiveness
Financial institutions should consider contingency plans for implementation
Outcomes must be measurable to demonstrate effectiveness and impact
Measurement methods must be appropriate for specific initiatives and relate directly to outcomes
Financial institutions must monitor and evaluate initiatives to assess effectiveness
Financial institutions must identify areas for improvement through monitoring
Financial institutions must collect data to improve future program effectiveness

sections.source_quote: A financial institution providing face-to-face financial education initiatives must utilise a location that is appropriate for this purpose to ensure that the financial education initiative is effective.
